Contradictory Test Case (Critical)\n39\t**Location:** `TestInt64ToUint64NegativeWithDifferentFormats`, test case \"int64 negative decimal format to uint64\"\n40\t\n41\t**Original (Malformed):**\n42\t```go\n43\t{\n44\t    name: \"int64 negative decimal format to uint64\",\n45\t    yamlContent: `\n46\tvalue: -100.0\n47\t`,\n48\t    target:      &struct{ Value uint64 }{},\n49\t    shouldError: false, // YAML parser handles decimals differently for uint64\n50\t    description: \"Negative decimal format should error for uint64\",  // ❌ Contradiction!\n51\t},\n52\t```\n53\t\n54\t**Fixed:**\n55\t```go\n56\t{\n57\t    name: \"int64 negative decimal format to uint64\",\n58\t    yamlContent: `\n59\tvalue: -100.0\n60\t`,\n61\t    target:      &struct{ Value uint64 }{},\n62\t    shouldError: false,\n63\t    description: \"Negative decimal format - YAML parser converts -100.0 to 100 for uint64 (differs from int32 behavior)\",  // ✅ Consistent\n64\t},\n65\t```\n66\t\n67\t**Issue:** The `shouldError: false` indicates the test should succeed, but the description says \"should error\" - a direct contradiction.\n68\t\n69\t---\n70\t\n71\t### 2. Missing expectedInMsg Values (Data Quality)\n72\t**Pattern:** Several test cases in the main `TestInt64ToUint64NegativeConversion` function had `expectedInMsg` arrays that only contained `\"cannot unmarshal\"` but should have included the specific negative value being tested.\n73\t\n74\t**Examples Fixed:**\n75\t\n76\t| Test Case | Original expectedInMsg | Fixed expectedInMsg |\n77\t|----------|----------------------|---------------------|\n78\t| \"int64 -2147483648 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-2147483648\"}` |\n79\t| \"int64 -4294967296 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-4294967296\"}` |\n80\t| \"int64 -65536 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-65536\"}` |\n81\t| \"int64 -32768 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-32768\"}` |\n82\t| \"int64 -256 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-256\"}` |\n83\t| \"int64 -128 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-128\"}` |\n84\t\n85\t**Reference Pattern (from int32 file):**\n86\t```go\n87\t{\n88\t    name: \"int32 -1 to uint32 should error\",\n89\t    yamlContent: `\n90\tvalue: -1\n91\t`,\n92\t    target:        &struct{ Value uint32 }{},\n93\t    shouldError:   true,\n94\t    description:   \"Negative value -1 cannot convert to uint32\",\n95\t    expectedInMsg: []string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-1\"},  // ✅ Includes specific value\n96\t},\n97\t```\n98\t\n99\t---\n100\t\n101\t### 3. Extra Test Cases Not Aligned with Pattern (Scope)\n102\t**Location:** `TestInt64ToUint64NegativeInNestedStructs`\n103\t\n104\t**Original had extra test cases not present in int32 file:**\n105\t\n106\t1. **\"int64 negative int32 minimum value\"** (lines 384-398)\n107\t   - Tests nested struct with int32 minimum value\n108\t   - Removed to align with int32 pattern\n109\t\n110\t2. **\"int64 negative in nested map of structs\"** (lines 400-418)\n111\t   - Tests nested map of structs with negative value\n112\t   - Removed to align with int32 pattern\n113\t\n114\t**Reference Pattern (int32 file):**\n115\tThe int32 file has only 5 test cases in `TestInt32ToUint32NegativeInNestedStructs`:\n116\t1. \"int32 negative in nested struct to uint32 field\"\n117\t2. \"int32 negative in array to uint32\"\n118\t3. \"int32 negative in map to uint32\"\n119\t4. \"int32 negative in slice of structs\"\n120\t5. \"int32 negative large value in nested struct\"\n121\t\n122\tThe corrected int64 file now matches this structure.\n123\t\n124\t---\n125\t\n126\t### 4. Main negative conversion tests\n2. **TestInt{XX}ToUint{XX}NegativeInNestedStructs** - Nested structure tests  \n3. **TestInt{XX}ToUint{XX}NegativeWithDifferentFormats** - Different format tests\n4. **TestInt{XX}ToUint{XX}BoundaryValues** - Boundary value tests\n5. **TestInt{XX}ToUint{XX}ErrorMessageQuality** - Error message quality tests\n\n#### Test Case Structure\nEach test case should include:\n```go\n{\n    name:          string,        // Descriptive test name\n    yamlContent:   string,        // YAML content to test\n    target:        interface{},    // Target struct for unmarshaling\n    shouldError:   bool,          // Whether error is expected\n    description:   string,        // Human-readable description\n    expectedInMsg: []string,      // Expected patterns in error message\n}\n```\n\n## Malformations Identified in Original int64 File\n\n### 1. Contradictory Test Case (Critical)\n**Location:** `TestInt64ToUint64NegativeWithDifferentFormats`, test case \"int64 negative decimal format to uint64\"\n\n**Original (Malformed):**\n```go\n{\n    name: \"int64 negative decimal format to uint64\",\n    yamlContent: `\nvalue: -100.0\n`,\n    target:      &struct{ Value uint64 }{},\n    shouldError: false, // YAML parser handles decimals differently for uint64\n    description: \"Negative decimal format should error for uint64\",  // ❌ Contradiction!\n},\n```\n\n**Fixed:**\n```go\n{\n    name: \"int64 negative decimal format to uint64\",\n    yamlContent: `\nvalue: -100.0\n`,\n    target:      &struct{ Value uint64 }{},\n    shouldError: false,\n    description: \"Negative decimal format - YAML parser converts -100.0 to 100 for uint64 (differs from int32 behavior)\",  // ✅ Consistent\n},\n```\n\n**Issue:** The `shouldError: false` indicates the test should succeed, but the description says \"should error\" - a direct contradiction.\n\n---\n\n### 2. Missing expectedInMsg Values (Data Quality)\n**Pattern:** Several test cases in the main `TestInt64ToUint64NegativeConversion` function had `expectedInMsg` arrays that only contained `\"cannot unmarshal\"` but should have included the specific negative value being tested.\n\n**Examples Fixed:**\n\n| Test Case | Original expectedInMsg | Fixed expectedInMsg |\n|----------|----------------------|---------------------|\n| \"int64 -2147483648 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-2147483648\"}` |\n| \"int64 -4294967296 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-4294967296\"}` |\n| \"int64 -65536 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-65536\"}` |\n| \"int64 -32768 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-32768\"}` |\n| \"int64 -256 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-256\"}` |\n| \"int64 -128 to uint64 should error\" | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\"}` | `[]string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-128\"}` |\n\n**Reference Pattern (from int32 file):**\n```go\n{\n    name: \"int32 -1 to uint32 should error\",\n    yamlContent: `\nvalue: -1\n`,\n    target:        &struct{ Value uint32 }{},\n    shouldError:   true,\n    description:   \"Negative value -1 cannot convert to uint32\",\n    expectedInMsg: []string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-1\"},  // ✅ Includes specific value\n},\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3. pattern:\n\n```go\nfunc TestInt{XX}ToUint{XX}NegativeConversion(t *testing.T) {\n    tests := []struct {\n        name          string\n        yamlContent   string\n        target        interface{}\n        shouldError   bool\n        description   string\n        expectedInMsg []string\n    }{\n        // Edge case: -1 (common negative value)\n        {\n            name: \"int{XX} -1 to uint{XX} should error\",\n            yamlContent: `\nvalue: -1\n`,\n            target:        &struct{ Value uint{XX} }{},\n            shouldError:   true,\n            description:   \"Negative value -1 cannot convert to uint{XX}\",\n            expectedInMsg: []string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"-1\"},  // ✅ Include specific value\n        },\n\n        // Edge case: minimum value\n        {\n            name: \"int{XX} {MIN_VALUE} to uint{XX} should error\",\n            yamlContent: `\nvalue: {MIN_VALUE}\n`,\n            target:        &struct{ Value uint{XX} }{},\n            shouldError:   true,\n            description:   \"Minimum int{XX} value {MIN_VALUE} cannot convert to uint{XX}\",\n            expectedInMsg: []string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"{MIN_VALUE}\"},  // ✅ Include specific value\n        },\n\n        // Additional negative values\n        {\n            name: \"int{XX} {VALUE} to uint{XX} should error\",\n            yamlContent: `\nvalue: {VALUE}\n`,\n            target:        &struct{ Value uint{XX} }{},\n            shouldError:   true,\n            description:   \"Negative value {VALUE} cannot convert to uint{XX}\",\n            expectedInMsg: []string{\"cannot unmarshal\", \"{VALUE}\"},  // ✅ Include specific value\n        },\n\n        // Positive values that should work\n        {\n            name: \"int{XX} 0 to uint{XX} should succeed\",\n            yamlContent: `\nvalue: 0\n`,\n            target:      &struct{ Value uint{XX} }{},\n            shouldError: false,\n            description: \"Zero successfully converts to uint{XX}\",\n        },\n    }\n\n    for _, tt := range tests {\n        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            parser := NewParser()\n            err := parser.ParseString(tt.yamlContent, tt.target)\n\n            if tt.shouldError {\n                if err == nil {\n                    t.Errorf(\"Test '%s' should error but didn't: %s\", tt.name, tt.description)\n                } else {\n                    t.Logf(\"✓ Test '%s' correctly produced error: %v\", tt.name, err)\n\n                    // Verify error message contains expected patterns\n                    errMsg := err.Error()\n                    lowerErrMsg := strings.ToLower(errMsg)\n\n                    for _, expected := range tt.expectedInMsg {\n %s\", expected, errMsg)\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    // Verify negative value indication in error\n                    if containsAny(lowerErrMsg, }\n                }\n            } else {\n                if err != nil {\n                    t.Errorf(\"Test '%s' should succeed but errored: %v - %s\", tt.name, err, tt.description)\n
